content:
type: QUESTION
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: I'm changing Editor.edit_file(filename) to Editor.edit_files(filenames) to support multi-file editing in click.edit(); will also touch edit() signature and subprocess call building the command line
blocked_on: need to know if your process_priority change touches edit_file's subprocess.Popen call signature so we don't clobber each other

fields.summary: I'm changing Editor.edit_file(filename) to Editor.edit_files(filenames) to support multi-file editing in click.edit(); will also touch edit() signature and subprocess call building the command line
fields.blocked_on: need to know if your process_priority change touches edit_file's subprocess.Popen call signature so we don't clobber each other

content:
type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Renamed edit_file(filename) to edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]); still ONE subprocess.Popen call per invocation (all filenames joined as separate quoted args on the same command line, not one Popen per file) - your popen_kwargs dict and _apply_unix_priority(c.pid) call can go right after that single Popen call unchanged. edit() in termui.py now normalizes filename (str or Iterable[str]) into a list and calls ed.edit_files(filenames=...). Will submit my patch.txt now; happy to adjust if your merge needs a different shape.

fields.summary: Renamed edit_file(filename) to edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]); still ONE subprocess.Popen call per invocation (all filenames joined as separate quoted args on the same command line, not one Popen per file) - your popen_kwargs dict and _apply_unix_priority(c.pid) call can go right after that single Popen call unchanged. edit() in termui.py now normalizes filename (str or Iterable[str]) into a list and calls ed.edit_files(filenames=...). Will submit my patch.txt now; happy to adjust if your merge needs a different shape.
